rule: All projects managed by codex-developer must have a package.json and a vercel.json file in the root directory before any deployment attempt.

VERCEL.JSON SCOPING: Before generating vercel.json, scan package.json and imports. Only include env references for services actually used (Clerk, Stripe, Supabase, Resend, PostHog, Sentry). If none are used, skip vercel.json entirely and let Vercel auto-detect the framework.

SUPABASE PACKAGE: For Next.js web projects, use '@supabase/supabase-js' and '@supabase/ssr' — NEVER the bare 'supabase' package (it's a CLI tool with native binaries that fail on Android ARM64 and aren't needed for web apps).
NEVER rewrite a working file from scratch. Always READ the current file first. Make surgical changes. If a feature already exists, skip it.
Before generating tasks, list all existing files and their key functions. Generate tasks only for MISSING features.
Maximum 40 lines changed per cycle. Use git diff to verify.
Always verify syntax before commit. On failure, revert and log.
Python imports: use module-relative imports (from models import X), NOT package-absolute (from backend.models import X) unless PYTHONPATH is set.
Python imports: use module-relative imports, not package-absolute, unless PYTHONPATH is set.
IMPORTS: Use correct import paths. If tools.py has web_search(), agent.py imports: from tools import web_search.
IMPORTS: Use correct import paths. main.py imports from agent.py and tools.py, does not redefine.
NO INVENTED IMPORTS: Only import from files that exist in the project or are standard libraries. If you need a component, create it first. Never import from paths that do not exist.

IMPORT RESOLUTION: Before using any import from @/components or ./ relative paths, verify the file exists. If importing from a file that does not exist, either CREATE that file first or REMOVE the import. Never leave broken imports.
NAMING: Match function/class names EXACTLY as specified. web_search not tool_web_search. Agent not run_agent.
NAMING: Match function/class names EXACTLY as specified.
NO DUPLICATION: Do not copy function definitions into multiple files. Each function lives in ONE file. Import it elsewhere.
NO DUPLICATION: Each function lives in ONE file. Import it elsewhere. Never redefine.
COMPLETENESS: Every function must have a real implementation. No pass, no TODO, no placeholder comments.
NO PLACEHOLDERS: Every function must have a real, working implementation. No pass, TODO, or placeholder text.
COMPLETE CONTENT: Every section you add must have real content. If adding Features, include actual feature descriptions. If adding a Hero, include real headline and subtext. No empty placeholders.
SELF-PROTECTION: Never modify files in the codex-developer skill directory.
SELF-PROTECTION: Never modify files in \$SKILLDIR.
NO MARKDOWN FENCES: Never wrap code output in ``` fences. Output raw code only. The first line of your response must be the actual code, not a markdown fence.
INCREMENTAL PATCHING: When modifying an existing file, read the FULL file first. Only change the requested sections. Keep ALL existing code intact. Do not remove working features.
WORKING LINKS: Every navigation link must point to an existing page or section. If the target does not exist, create it. No dead links.
ERROR RESILIENCE: Wrap dynamic imports and auth checks in try/catch. If a service is unavailable, show a graceful fallback message instead of crashing the page.

ERROR BOUNDARIES: For React/Next.js pages, wrap the main component in an error boundary. Use try/catch in the default export. Show a graceful fallback message instead of crashing the page on import errors.

FOR WEB PROJECTS: Use Next.js 16 App Router with TypeScript. Use Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI for styling. Use Supabase for database/auth. Use Clerk for authentication. Use Recharts for charts. Use pnpm as package manager.
STACK DETECTION: If the project has package.json, it IS a web project and MUST use the VibeStack. If no package.json, determine the appropriate stack from the request.
ANDROID PLATFORM: When building Next.js projects on Android/Termux (aarch64), use Next.js 16.x which has WASM fallback. Version 14.x SWC binaries are not available for Android.
NO UNNECESSARY DEPENDENCIES: Do not import from packages not in package.json. For theme providers, use a simple pass-through wrapper. Never add imports that require npm install.
NEXT.JS CLIENT COMPONENTS: Every file using React hooks MUST start with "use client" as the first line. Server components cannot use hooks. Without this directive, the build fails.
TSCONFIG AUTO-GENERATION: For Next.js projects, always create tsconfig.json with paths: {@/*: [./*]}. This enables @/components imports.
CLERK PROVIDER REQUIRED: Any page or component using Clerk hooks MUST have a parent layout.tsx with <ClerkProvider> wrapping children.
CLERK PROXY: For Next.js 16+ with Clerk, proxy.ts MUST export default clerkMiddleware from @clerk/nextjs/server. NEVER create custom proxy handlers.
CLERK NEXT.JS 16 INCOMPATIBILITY: Clerk v5 does NOT work with Next.js 16. Use guest mode as fallback. Or use NextAuth.js instead of Clerk.
NATIVE MODULE BLOCKLIST: Never include these npm packages: canvas, sharp, puppeteer, playwright, node-gyp, bcrypt, sqlite3. These require native compilation and fail on Android.
FILE EXTRACTION FILTER: Never extract bare version numbers, framework names, or garbage tokens as filenames. Only extract strings that look like actual file paths with extensions.
PACKAGE.JSON GENERATION: For any Node.js/Next.js project, ALWAYS create package.json with proper dependencies before building other files.
DEPENDENCY PRECISION: Only include packages that are actually imported in the project. Never add native-compilation packages for browser functionality.

PRE-FLIGHT AUDIT: Before proposing any code modification or import structure, perform a mandatory audit of the actual filesystem (e.g., using 'ls -R '). All proposed imports must map to existing files discovered in this audit. If a module is missing, create it first before importing it. Strictly enforce this to prevent ModuleNotFoundError/hallucinations.
